head.text: Congestion of head and chest; coma. Contraction of one pupil and dilatation of the other. Sopor, eyes half open red face
Great congestion of head and chest.

extremities.text: Jerking of limbs. Trembling of whole body. Violent convulsions. Limbs and head fall over as if paralyzed. Extremities cold. Cold perspiration

keynotes.text: color="#800000">LONICERA XYLOSTEUM Fly-woodbine Convulsive symptoms. Uræmic convulsions. Albuminuria. Syphilis.

Lon. xylost. is known from the effect of poisoning by its berries. Vomiting and purging; trembling and jerking of the limbs, convulsions and coma were the chief symptoms. Peculiar symptoms were: Contraction of one pupil and dilatation of the other. Abdomen retracted especially in umbilical region. Slow pulse.

generalities.text: Respiration rapid and deep. Frequent jerking of limbs. Trembling and jerking of whole body. Violent convulsions. Seemed neither to hear nor see; unable to make answer. When raised from bed, limbs and head fall over as if paralysed.

_raw: Compare: Dig. (slow pulse). Cad. s. and Rhod. (one pupil contracted, the other dilated). For botan. relations see Lon. peri.

eyes.text: Conjunctiva red. Pupils dilated. Contraction of one pupil, dilatation of the other. Everything foggy and indistinct to vision for half an hour. Photophobia.

stomach_abdomen.text: Excessive thirst. Profuse vomiting; and purging. Abdomen retracted, esp. in umbilical region; soft. Violent colic.

chest_heart.text: Violent impulse of heart. Pulse: slow; small, soft, irregular.

sleep.text: Sleepy. Constant deep sleep with half-open eyes; red face.

fever_chill.text: Extremities cold. Fever. Profuse cold sweat. Copyright © Médi-T ® 2000 Main.
